sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 29

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 29:1
items.html: (to the question) For "Bina extends until Hod," and this is drawn from the beginning of the shaping of the Kelim [vessels] in AB of AK [Adam Kadmon]: in the Sefira of Hod, Behina Aleph was refined, and the level of Hassadim — which is the Light of Bina — departed from the Partzuf, and nothing remained for the Yesod but the Light of Malchut. Therefore a lack of Hassadim from the extension of Bina was engraved in the Kli of Yesod. And since every Kli comprises three thirds, whose source is the discernments of GE [Galgalta ve Eynaim] above the second Tzimtzum [restriction] — Keter, Hochma, and the GAR of Bina — which emerge at the time of the Katnut of every Partzuf, whereas the AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh] that come with the Gadlut are not the essence of the Partzuf, being passing and returning, as is known — therefore these three thirds were set in every Sefira. But the Yesod, which has no root in Bina, remained lacking the lower third of Bina; and so it received only the discernment of the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey] that was included in Bina, and it became its lower third — in the secret of the verse "and comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure." (p. 762, Item 46)
